mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces equity discount rates, compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, equity markets price in prolonged recessionary drag and weak corporate earnings, causing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity-constrained environment, sharp monthly declines in industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning and margin calls, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances following INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation stabilizes above 210, real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity discount rates and driving positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances over a rolling 36-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive payroll revisions fail to predict positive 20-day returns at statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent signals stalled economic momentum and weak aggregate demand, triggering immediate portfolio de-risking and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll employment falls indicates capital-intensive restructuring rather than genuine demand recovery, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of instances showing this specific supply-labor divergence pattern.
